taskDescription: Click on all the bones of the upper limb mentioned in the text below.
textField: The skeleton of the upper limb consists of the pectoral girdle and the free upper limb. The *clavicle* is the only long bone that lies horizontally and is the most commonly fractured bone in the body. The *scapula* is a flat bone that forms the posterior part of the shoulder girdle. The *humerus* is the single long bone of the arm, connecting the shoulder to the elbow. In the forearm, the *radius* is lateral and the *ulna* is medial. The wrist contains eight carpal bones arranged in two rows: the proximal row includes the *scaphoid*, lunate, triquetral, and *pisiform*. A fall on an outstretched hand commonly fractures the scaphoid, which can lead to avascular necrosis because its blood supply enters distally.
